title: Pre-16 schools funding: local authority guidance for 2026 to 2027
body: "Schools funding operational guide: 2026 to 2027  Local authorities and schools forums should use this guidance to plan the local implementation of the mainstream schools funding system for the 2026 to 2027 financial year.  The notional SEN budget: operational guidance  This guidance has been written to help local authorities comply with the notional special educational needs (SEN) budget requirement. Local authorities are required to identify a notional amount to guide mainstream schools in their spending to meet the costs of additional support for the school’s pupils with SEN.  Growth and falling rolls fund guidance: 2026 to 2027  Local authorities should use this guidance when operating funds for growth or falling rolls – providing some more detail above what is in the schools revenue funding operational guide: 2026 to 2027. The guidance includes examples of compliant criteria.  Private finance initiative (PFI) factor in the schools national funding formula guidance  Guidance for mainstream schools with pupils aged 5 to 16, and local authorities, to understand the operation of the PFI factor in the schools NFF, and the equivalent local authority funding formulae.  Private finance initiative (PFI) affordability model template  Local authorities can use this template to model the amount of funding allocated through the PFI factor in the schools NFF.  Allowable factor value ranges: 2026 to 2027  We have published a spreadsheet which shows the allowable factor value ranges in schools funding formulae for each local authority in 2026 to 2027.  Guidance for completing the disapplications digital form  Local authorities should use the disapplication digital form to submit their disapplication requests. This guidance provides further information on how to complete and submit the form.  All safety valve local authorities should contact SafetyValve.Programme@education.gov.uk to discuss the appropriate route for submitting requests before proceeding.  Local authorities should refer to the ‘Transfer between blocks’ section of the ‘Schools funding operational guide: 2026 to 2027’ for further information on block transfer requests, including details of the supporting evidence we would expect to be submitted as part of the request.  Further information  You can read the high needs funding 2026 to 2027: operational guide for information for local authorities, schools and colleges about high needs funding arrangements for the 2026 to 2027 financial and academic year.  You can read the national funding formula for schools policy document for further details of the NFF allocations for 2026 to 2027.  You can view the provisional national funding formula allocation tables for schools and central school services blocks for 2026 to 2027.  The authority proforma tool (APT) is designed to support local authorities in modelling different options for their funding formulae. It is also how local authorities submit details of their schools funding formulae to us, in accordance with arrangements set out by the Secretary of State for Education. The information submitted in the APT also allows us to calculate academy budgets and recoupment deductions to dedicated schools grant allocations. The APT for 2026 to 2027 and guidance on how to complete the APT is now available for local authorities to download.  The School and Early Years Finance (England) Regulations 2025 details statutory guidance for local authorities funding arrangements of their maintained schools and early years providers...

taxons.title: Funding for different types of schools and settings
